mechanism: Azure Policy denies cross-tenant object replication at the management group level. Exceptions require a policy exemption.
why: Cross-tenant replication operates at the data plane — network deny policies don't intercept it. Without explicit denial, data is replicable to external tenants.

related.note: Network deny removes the public endpoint but not cross-tenant replication — both denies required independently.
Must use a Deny-prefixed assignment for cross-tenant replication compliance.
Self-service exemptions make hard denies viable.
A compliant default does not substitute for a Deny gate.

violations: Storage account with cross-tenant object replication enabled.
Replication rule targeting an account in a different tenant via exemption without justification.
